meta.access: OPERATOR-FACING ONLY — never expose in player lobby, player-facing API, or any UI visible to players. This is the P4 eval harness answer key.
meta.purpose: Ground-truth labels for the three mandatory demo cases (PRD §6) and the seven seeded eval cases (PRD §7). P4 eval harness checks that true-risk cases rank above false-positive traps and that AI summaries do not overclaim.

prd_refs.contracts: PRD §4 Contract 1 (produced by P2), Contract 3 (evidence packet schema, defined by P4)

cases.prd_ref: PRD §6 New Player Case / §7 Eval A

counterfactual.standard_room_outcome: P-104 joins T-22; short session (~12 min); likely churn
counterfactual.fairplay_outcome: P-104 routed to T-8; longer session (~42 min expected); retained

cases.note: P-164 is a strong, high-volume grinder (285k lifetime hands, 360 min avg sessions). Health concern: may drive casuals off tables. NOT an integrity risk — no device link, no cluster, no soft-play. Eval must confirm the system does not over-escalate skilled-but-legal play.

cases.prd_ref: PRD §6 Coordinated Cluster Case / §7 Eval C
counter_evidence: no single signal alone is proof of collusion
device link could be shared workstation
timing correlation could reflect same time zone / schedule
co-seating could reflect preference for same stake level

counterfactual.standard_room_outcome: P-200 (Account C) completes cluster formation; cluster plays as coordinated unit; high casual-exit impact
counterfactual.fairplay_outcome: P-200 held for pit-boss review; cluster formation blocked pending decision; pit boss accepts containment

distinguishing_signals.explanation: same preferred stakes and time slot

cases.note: P-142 and P-143 co-seat 50% of recent sessions — high overlap by count, but entirely missing the corroborating signals (device, soft-play, profitability correlation) that would make it suspicious. A classifier that escalates this is over-triggering.

cases.prd_ref: PRD §6 Shared-Device False Positive / §7 Eval E

counterfactual.standard_room_outcome: H1/H2 play normally; no suppression needed
counterfactual.fairplay_outcome: H1/H2 monitored only; no intervention; FairPlay correctly avoids false positive

distinguishing_signals.pattern: completes minimum then leaves — high churn, promo drain, not collusion

cases.note: P-184 exploits promotional structures — 21 redemptions in 30 days, 32-min average sessions (just enough to qualify). This is a distinct pattern from collusion. Scoring and AI must place this in a promo-abuse bucket, not an integrity-review bucket.

distinguishing_signals.note: Near-daily play, extremely consistent session length and timing — high bot_similarity_score from simulated composite. Low vpip (0.24) and high sessions_last_30d create an unusual combination for a player registered only 95 days ago.

cases.note: P-221 triggers on bot_similarity_score and timing_regularity alone — no device link, no cluster, no soft-play. Must be routed to its own review queue distinct from collusion/cluster cases. Demonstrates the system's ability to distinguish integrity sub-categories.

counterfactual_scenario.shared_starting_state: Identical room configuration at hour 0 for both paths

standard_room.label: Standard Room — no FairPlay intervention
key_events: P-104 joins T-22 (high-volatility, beginner-unfriendly); expected session ~12 min before churn
P-200 (Account C) completes cluster CL-001 formation at active table; cluster plays as coordinated unit
No risk-aware routing; no pit-boss hold; no table suppression for new/recreational players

fairplay_enabled.label: FairPlay Enabled — interventions active
key_events: P-104 routed to T-8 (balanced, healthy alternative); expected session ~42 min; retained
P-200 held for pit-boss review before completing cluster CL-001 formation; pit boss accepts containment recommendation
T-22 not promoted to new or recreational players in lobby during simulation window

eval_summary.eval_invariant: True-risk cases must rank above false-positive traps in integrity and seating-risk scores. Eval fails if a trap (B, D, or E) scores higher than its paired true-risk case in the same risk lens.

schema_warnings.table_id_format: RESOLVED 2026-06-20. All table-label join keys now match data/table_roster.json's exact string format: CASE-A uses T-22 (seeded), T-8 (healthy alternative), T-14 (good fit); CASE-C uses T-11 (cluster CL-001 table, replaces former 'T-cluster' placeholder). Format is the 'T-<n>' string with no leading zeros (roster has T-1/T-3/T-5/T-7/T-8/T-9/T-11/T-14/T-15/T-18/T-22/T-25). P3 joins on these keys against table_roster.json are safe.
schema_warnings.room_metrics_files: data/room_metrics_standard.json and data/room_metrics_fairplay.json are required Contract 1 outputs owned by Cleo (P2c). They are not present yet — Cleo must generate them. These are required for the simulator comparison screen (PRD §5 P1 Room Impact Simulator, PRD §6 Counterfactual runs).
